templates.name: Millennial vs Gen Z Split
templates.description: Two-panel split ad contrasting verbose millennial-style copy against minimal Gen Z emoji language for the same product — a viral social engagement format seen across Starbucks, Edgars Fashion, and Comfycup.
templates.prompt:
Create a square 1:1 ad creative using a split-panel "Millennial vs Gen Z" format.

LAYOUT:
Two equal vertical panels side by side, filling the full frame. A thin centre divider line (1–2px, neutral mid-grey) separates the panels. Each panel is visually self-contained. The overall composition is symmetrical and balanced. Small generation label pills sit at the top of each panel.

BACKGROUND:
Both panels share a clean white or off-white background (#FFFFFF or #F7F7F7). No heavy gradients or textures — the contrast comes entirely from the copy, not the visual environment.

PRODUCT:
The exact same product is shown centred in both panels, identically cropped and scaled. Product photography should be clean studio style — flat or lightly lit, with a subtle drop shadow or none. No hands or lifestyle scene. The product occupies roughly the middle third of each panel.

TEXT BLOCKS:
Left panel — Millennial side:
- Label pill: MILLENNIAL — small rounded pill/badge near top-centre of panel, neutral grey fill, white text, small font size
- Headline: [INSERT MILLENNIAL HEADLINE] — 8–14 words, slightly formal or elaborate language, medium-weight sans-serif or light serif, dark colour (#1A1A1A), placed above or below the product
- Body copy: [INSERT MILLENNIAL COPY] — 2–3 lines of detailed, benefit-driven, adjective-heavy language (e.g. "precision-crafted", "elevated sensory experience"), small font size, left- or centre-aligned

Right panel — Gen Z side:
- Label pill: GEN Z — small rounded pill/badge near top-centre of panel, matching or slightly brighter fill, same text treatment as left pill
- Headline: [INSERT GEN Z HEADLINE] — 1–5 words maximum, ultra-casual language, may include 1–2 relevant emojis, bold sans-serif, same dark colour
- Body copy: [INSERT GEN Z COPY] — a single emoji or ultra-short phrase (2–4 words), large or centre-aligned, generous whitespace

Bottom of image (spanning full width):
- Brand logo: [INSERT BRAND NAME / LOGO] — centred, small and clean, below both panels. Optional thin horizontal rule separating it from the panels.

BRAND ELEMENTS:
Logo centred at the base spanning the full width. No heavy decorative elements — restraint is essential. The humour lives in the copy contrast, not the design.

COLOUR PALETTE:
- Background: #FFFFFF or #F7F7F7
- Primary text (both panels): #1A1A1A
- Label pills: brand accent colour or #E0E0E0 neutral grey
- Divider line: #CCCCCC
- Accent: [INSERT BRAND ACCENT COLOUR]

STYLE NOTES:
Clean, contemporary, deliberately self-aware and slightly ironic. The post is engineered for social engagement — the split format is the hook that drives "which side are you?" comments. Keep the design neutral and restrained; any visual noise competes with the copy contrast. Product photography must match exactly between panels. Do not add lifestyle backgrounds, gradients, or decorative shapes.

Output: single square 1:1 image, 2000×2000px, photorealistic studio quality.
templates.reverse_engineered_from: Instagram trend — Comfycup.ph, Edgars Fashion, Starbucks India: Millennial vs Gen Z split-panel posts, May 2026
